old_claim.note: Early Superconductors is a plausible dependency for a forecast technology and should be treated as speculative.

new_claim.note: High-temperature superconductors are a later branch of superconductivity research; earlier superconductivity was discovered in 1911 and frames the temperature-limit breakthrough.

source_shape.source_locator: Background Information section starts from the 1911 discovery before describing the 1986 high-temperature breakthrough.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The 1986 result exceeded prior superconducting-temperature limits in an established superconductivity research lineage.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports historical_predecessor, not speculative forecast dependency.

ontology_before: The graph treated high-temperature superconductors as a 2035 forecast connected speculatively to early superconductors.
ontology_after: The graph treats early superconductivity as the historical predecessor to an established 1986 material class.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to future room-temperature superconductors rather than established high-Tc materials.
A narrower high-Tc cuprates node is later added and this broad node is split.
short_reason: High-Tc superconductors are a 1986 branch of superconductivity research.
